{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 1287 would create a new program in California to allow for the building of more units for middle-income earners.",
    "AB 1287 creates a new incentive for the development of Middle Income Housing.",
    "According to the Public Policy Institute of California, 47 percent of Californians cite housing costs as a significant financial strain on themselves and their families.",
    "One in three Californians say they are seriously considering leaving the state because of housing costs.",
    "40% of middle-income renters spend more than 30% of their income on housing.",
    "10% of middle-income renters are severely cost-burdened, meaning that more than half of their monthly income is spent on housing.",
    "The density bonus law has been successful in creating new housing across California.",
    "AB 1287 would help increase the numbers of new housing units and create a new avenue for middle-income housing."
  ]
}
